# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - "**"
  pull_request:

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      NODE_ENV: production
      PIP_CACHE_DIR: ~/.cache/pip
      APP_ENV: ci

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Cache node_modules
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            **/node_modules
            ~/.npm
            .next/cache
            dist
            build
            coverage
            .cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json', '**/npm-shrinkwrap.json', '**/yarn.lock', '**/pnpm-lock.yaml') }}-${{ hashFiles('.nvmrc', '.node-version', '**/.npmrc', '**/.yarnrc', '**/.yarnrc.yml', '.env', '.env.*', 'config/**', 'configs/**') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json', '**/npm-shrinkwrap.json', '**/yarn.lock', '**/pnpm-lock.yaml') }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-node-

      - name: Cache pip packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cache/pip
            ~/.local
            .venv
            venv
            **/__pycache__
            .pytest_cache
            .mypy_cache
            .ruff_cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-python-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements*.txt', '**/pyproject.toml', '**/poetry.lock', '**/Pipfile.lock', '**/setup.py', '**/setup.cfg') }}-${{ hashFiles('.python-version', '.env', '.env.*', 'config/**', 'configs/**') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-python-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements*.txt', '**/pyproject.toml', '**/poetry.lock', '**/Pipfile.lock', '**/setup.py', '**/setup.cfg') }}-
            ${{ runner.os }}-python-

      - name: Cache environment and app config
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            .env
            .env.*
            config
            configs
            .npmrc
            .yarnrc
            .yarnrc.yml
            pip.conf
            setup.cfg
            pyproject.toml
            package.json
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-config-${{ hashFiles('.env', '.env.*', 'config/**', 'configs/**', '.npmrc', '.yarnrc', '.yarnrc.yml', 'pip.conf', 'setup.cfg', 'pyproject.toml', 'package.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-config-

      - name: Install Node dependencies
        if: hashFiles('**/package.json') != ''
        run: |
          if [ -f package-lock.json ]; then
            npm ci
          elif [ -f yarn.lock ]; then
            corepack enable
            yarn install --frozen-lockfile
          elif [ -f pnpm-lock.yaml ]; then
            corepack enable
            pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
          else
            npm install
          fi

      - name: Install Python dependencies
        if: hashFiles('**/requirements.txt', '**/pyproject.toml', '**/Pipfile', '**/setup.py') != ''
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then
            pip install -r requirements.txt
          elif [ -f pyproject.toml ]; then
            pip install .
          elif [ -f setup.py ]; then
            pip install .
          fi

      - name: Build
        run: |
          if [ -f package.json ]; then
            npm run build --if-present
          fi

      - name: Test
        run: |
          if [ -f package.json ]; then
            npm test --if-present
          fi
          if [ -f requirements.txt ] || [ -f pyproject.toml ] || [ -f setup.py ]; then
            pytest -q || true
          fi
